name: Lamprey (SS-372)
events: 17 Dec 1944 USS Lamprey (Cdr. W.T. Nelson, USN) departed New Orleans, Louisiana for the Panama Canal Zone.
22 Dec 1944 USS Lamprey (Cdr. W.T. Nelson, USN) arrived in the Panama Canal Zone from New Orleans, Louisiana.
13 Jan 1945 After a training period, USS Lamprey (Cdr. W.T. Nelson, USN), departed the Panama Canal Zone for Pearl Harbor.

5 Feb 1945 USS Bugara (Cdr. A.F. Schade, USN) conducted exercises off Pearl Harbor together with USS Pruitt (Cdr. P.H. Bjarnason, USN), USS Whitman (Lt. E.A. Sandor, USNR) and USS Lamprey (Cdr. W.T. Nelson, USN).
17 Feb 1945 USS Lamprey (Cdr. W.T. Nelson) departed from Pearl Harbor for her 1st war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in off Formosa and in the South China Sea.
22 Apr 1945 USS Lamprey (Cdr. W.T. Nelson) ended her 1st war patrol at Fremantle, Australia.
21 May 1945 USS Lamprey (Lt.Cdr. L.B. McDonald) departed from Fremantle for her 2nd war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in the Gulf of Siam.
28 May 1945 USS Lamprey (Lt.Cdr. L.B. McDonald) damaged an unidentified Japanese vessel in a surface gunnery action off Japara, Netherlands East Indies in position 06°28'S, 110°37'E.

14 Jun 1945 At 2307 H/14, orders were received from CTF 71 that USS Icefish (T/Cdr. R.W. Peterson, USN) was to take charge of a coordinated attack group which besides herself was made up of USS Lamprey (T/Lt.Cdr. L.B. McDonald, USN) and HMS Selene (Lt.Cdr. H.R.B. Newton, DSC, RN). (1)
29 Jun 1945 USS Lamprey (Lt.Cdr. L.B. McDonald) ended her 2nd war patrol at Subic Bay, Philippines.
26 Jul 1945 USS Lamprey (Lt.Cdr. L.B. McDonald) departed from Subic Bay for her 3rd war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in the Gulf of Siam.
8 Aug 1945 USS Lamprey (Lt.Cdr. L.B. McDonald) sank a small Japanese vessel with gunfire off Singapore.

11 Aug 1945 At 0120 I/11, USS Kraken (T/Cdr. T.H. Henry, USN) made rendezvous with USS Lamprey (T/Lt.Cdr. L.B. McDonald, USN). It was arranged that USS Lamprey would patrol the area to the east of the Thousand Islands and that USS Kraken would patrol to the west of the Thousand Islands and the coast of Sumatra until the 15th when the two submarines would change stations. (2)
12 Aug 1945 USS Lamprey (Lt.Cdr. L.B. McDonald) sank a small Japanese vessel with gunfire off the north coast of Java in approximate position 06°00'S, 108°00'E.
20 Aug 1945 USS Lamprey (Lt.Cdr. L.B. McDonald) ended her 3rd war patrol at Subic Bay.

31 Aug 1945 The submarine tender USS Howard W. Gilmore (T/Cdr. C.A. Johnson, USN) and the submarines USS Puffer (T/Lt.Cdr. C.R. Dwyer, USN), USS Rasher (T/Lt.Cdr. C.D. Nace, USN), USS Raton (T/Lt.Cdr. G.F. Gugliotta, USN), USS Baya (T/Lt.Cdr. B.C. Jarvis, USN), USS Becuna (T/Lt.Cdr. W.J. Bush, USN), USS Caiman (T/Cdr. W.L. Fey, Jr., USN), USS Blenny (T/Lt.Cdr. C.H. Fuller, Jr., USN), USS Blueback (T/Cdr. M.K. Clementson, USN), USS Boarfish (T/Lt.Cdr. E.C. Blonts, Jr., USN), USS Capitaine (T/Lt.Cdr. E.S. Friedrick, USN), USS Carbonero (T/Cdr. C.L. Murphy, USN), USS Entemedor (T/Cdr. W.R. Smith, Jr., USN), USS Hardhead (T/Lt.Cdr. J.L. Haines, USN), USS Hawkbill (T/Cdr. W. Scanland, Jr., USN), USS Kraken (T/Cdr. T.H. Henry, USN), USS Lamprey (T/Lt.Cdr. L.B. McDonald, USN), USS Lizardfish (T/Cdr. O.M. Butler, USN) and USS Loggerhead (T/Cdr. R.N. Metcalf, USN) departed Subic Bay. All were to proceed to Pearl Harbour except USS Blenny, USS Blueback and USS Boarfish which were to proceed to Guam.

14 Sep 1945 The submarine tender USS USS Howard W. Gilmore (T/Cdr. C.A. Johnson, USN) and the submarines USS Puffer (T/Lt.Cdr. C.R. Dwyer, USN), USS Rasher (T/Lt.Cdr. C.D. Nace, USN), USS Raton (T/Lt.Cdr. G.F. Gugliotta, USN), USS Baya (T/Lt.Cdr. B.C. Jarvis, USN), USS Becuna (T/Lt.Cdr. W.J. Bush, USN), USS Caiman (T/Cdr. W.L. Fey, Jr., USN), USS Capitaine (T/Lt.Cdr. E.S. Friedrick, USN), USS Carbonero (T/Cdr. C.L. Murphy, USN), USS Entemedor (T/Cdr. W.R. Smith, Jr., USN), USS Hardhead (T/Lt.Cdr. J.L. Haines, USN), USS Hawkbill (T/Cdr. W. Scanland, Jr., USN), USS Kraken (T/Cdr. T.H. Henry, USN), USS Lamprey (T/Lt.Cdr. L.B. McDonald, USN), USS Lizardfish (T/Cdr. O.M. Butler, USN) and USS Loggerhead (T/Cdr. R.N. Metcalf, USN) arrived at Pearl Harbor from Subic Bay.

15 Sep 1945 USS Hardhead (T/Lt.Cdr. J.L. Haines, USN), USS Hawkbill (T/Cdr. W. Scanland, Jr., USN), USS Kraken (T/Cdr. T.H. Henry, USN), USS Lamprey (T/Lt.Cdr. L.B. McDonald, USN), USS Lizardfish (T/Cdr. O.M. Butler, USN) and USS Loggerhead (T/Cdr. R.N. Metcalf, USN) departed Pearl Harbor for San Francisco.

22 Sep 1945 USS Hardhead (T/Lt.Cdr. J.L. Haines, USN), USS Hawkbill (T/Cdr. W. Scanland, Jr., USN), USS Kraken (T/Cdr. T.H. Henry, USN), USS Lamprey (T/Lt.Cdr. L.B. McDonald, USN), USS Lizardfish (T/Cdr. O.M. Butler, USN) and USS Loggerhead (T/Cdr. R.N. Metcalf, USN) arrived at San Francisco from Pearl Harbor.
